violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Person in charge showed proof that he is scheduled to take the course on 8/28/23. Please email inspector proof of course completion. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2023

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Per person in charge, he has taken the course but needs to retake the test. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2023

Soup was observed held at 44 degrees Fahrenheit in the refrigerator. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Soup was placed in the freezer to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings behind equipment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Per person in charge, pest control services will be contacted. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2023

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2023

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. The person in charge showed proof of enrollment in a food safety course for 7/18/23. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice was disposed of (see attached disposal form). Ice machine was taken out of service and must not be used until it is cleaned. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

Food was observed held at 44-47 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been in the refrigerator since the day prior. Food was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Refrigerator had an ambient air temperature of 47 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Refrigerator was taken out of service and must not be used until it can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Establishment must repair/replace or adjust temperature. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

Food was observed held at 44-46 degrees Fahrenheit in prep unit #1. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been in the unit since the day prior. Food was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). The ambient air temperature of the prep unit was 44 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Prep unit #1 was taken out of service and must not be used until it can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Prep unit #1 must be repaired/replaced or adjusted. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

Thermometers are lacking in the refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings behind equipment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Pest control services must be scheduled. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

Ice machine located in the kitchen area was not in good repair. Hardware is rusted and must be replaced. The handle on the prep unit is missing and must be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

The floors and walls throughout the facility, especially under grill area, have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

The walk-in cooler in the kitchen area is in poor repair. Condenser is dripping. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Food in the dripping radius was relocated during time of inspection. Walk-in cooler must be repaired. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

The mop sink in the kitchen area is in poor repair. Sink has a crack and leaks. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Mop sink must be repaired. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023
Menus are lacking an allergy statement notifying customers of their obligation to notify staff if they have a food allergy. New Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2023

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Please email inspector proof of enrollment in a food safety course. New Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice was disposed at time of inspection. The person in charge purchased bagged ice during time of inspection. Ice machine must not be used until it is cleaned. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2023

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2023

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. New Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2023
Sinks do not have hot water. Per person in charge, hot water will be turned on. New Violation. Correct By: 06/14/2023
The receptacle in the women's toilet room is lacking a cover. A toilet room used by females shall be provided with a covered receptacle for sanitary napkins. New Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2023

The woman's toilet is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Establishment has two additional toilets in the women's restroom that are in good repair. Establishment placed an out of order sign on the toilet during time of inspection. Toilet must be repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2023

The ceiling tiles in the kitchen area are in poor repair. Water marks were observed on several tiles and several ceiling tiles were missing. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2023
The perimeter of the walk-in cooler and vent above the dishwasher have exposed insulation. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2023

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/21/2023
